problem.title: Accepted input parser 1
problem.description: Read n and n integers, then output their sum.
commonMistakes: This fixture checks that similar code is not over-diagnosed.

submission.sourceCode:
n = int(input())
a = list(map(int, input().split()))
print(sum(a))

baseline.studentHint: Keep the diagnosis conservative and review why this code should not receive the forbidden label.

quality.misconception: The analyzer may confuse ordinary input parsing with an input-format bug.
quality.expectedStudentMove: Treat normal parsing code as background evidence only.

problem.title: Accepted DP previous state 1
problem.description: Count ways to climb n steps using one or two steps each time.
commonMistakes: This fixture checks that similar code is not over-diagnosed.

submission.sourceCode:
n = int(input())
dp = [0] * (n + 1)
dp[0] = 1
for i in range(1, n + 1):
    dp[i] += dp[i - 1]
    if i >= 2:
        dp[i] += dp[i - 2]
print(dp[n])

baseline.studentHint: Keep the diagnosis conservative and review why this code should not receive the forbidden label.

quality.misconception: The analyzer may treat normal dp[i - 1] transitions as off-by-one.
quality.expectedStudentMove: Recognize previous-state references as normal DP evidence.

problem.title: Accepted accumulator 1
problem.description: Read n and output the sum from 0 to n - 1.
commonMistakes: This fixture checks that similar code is not over-diagnosed.

submission.sourceCode:
n = int(input())
answer = 0
for i in range(n):
    answer += i
print(answer)

baseline.studentHint: Keep the diagnosis conservative and review why this code should not receive the forbidden label.

quality.misconception: The analyzer may treat a normal zero accumulator as a bad initial state.
quality.expectedStudentMove: Only promote initial-state labels when the variable role makes zero suspicious.

problem.title: Accepted hidden pass 1
problem.description: Accepted code with visible and hidden cases all passing.
commonMistakes: This fixture checks that similar code is not over-diagnosed.

submission.sourceCode:
x = int(input())
print("YES" if x % 2 == 0 else "NO")

baseline.studentHint: Keep the diagnosis conservative and review why this code should not receive the forbidden label.

quality.misconception: The analyzer may confuse hidden-case presence with hidden-case failure.
quality.expectedStudentMove: Only failed hidden cases should support sample-overfit concerns.

problem.title: TLE is not sample overfit 1
problem.description: A slow but general simulation reaches the time limit on large input.
commonMistakes: This fixture checks that similar code is not over-diagnosed.

submission.sourceCode:
n = int(input())
total = 0
for i in range(n):
    total += i
print(total)

baseline.studentHint: Keep the diagnosis conservative and review why this code should not receive the forbidden label.

quality.misconception: The analyzer may label every hidden failure as sample overfit, even for TLE.
quality.expectedStudentMove: Keep TLE diagnosis at complexity level unless code shape gives stronger fine evidence.

problem.title: Accepted dedupe intended 1
problem.description: Read n ids and output the number of distinct ids.
commonMistakes: This fixture checks that similar code is not over-diagnosed.

submission.sourceCode:
n = int(input())
ids = list(map(int, input().split()))
print(len(set(ids)))

baseline.studentHint: Keep the diagnosis conservative and review why this code should not receive the forbidden label.

quality.misconception: The analyzer may assume every set() use destroys required duplicates.
quality.expectedStudentMove: Use the problem statement before promoting dedupe to a duplicate-case bug.
